title: ChartTile
description: A tile that queries data and renders a chart.

layout.description:
Information about how the tile is placed on the dashboard.

Examples:
- a tile occupying the first half of the top row: `{ "position": { "col": 0, "row": 0 }, size: { "cols": 3, "rows": 1 } }`
- a tile occupying the second half of the top row: `{ "position": { "col": 3, "row": 0 }, size: { "cols": 3, "rows": 1 } }`

position.description: Position of the tile in the dashboard's grid.  Numbering starts at 0, so a tile in the upper left of the dashboard will be at column 0, row 0.

size.description: Number of columns and rows the tile occupies.  A dashboard always has 6 columns, but has as many rows as needed to accommodate the given tiles.

type.description: The type of tile.  Chart tiles must have type 'chart'.

definition.description:
The tile's definition, which consists of a query to fetch data and a chart to render the data.
Note that some charts expect certain types of queries to render properly.  The documentation for the individual chart types has more information.
